zh.North Wyke, South Tawton, Devon, England: 英國英格蘭戴文素斯 塔福頓
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Wykes於1217年出生在英國英格蘭戴文素斯 塔福頓。她於大約1242年與William Wykes在英國戴文維可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1248年之後。

nameMeanings.name: Wykes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire, Northamptonshire, and Warwickshire): variant of   Wicks  .

variants.name: Wicks
meanings: English (southern England and East Anglia): generally a variant of Wick , Wyke , or the obsolete form Week, with post-medieval excrescent -s. Occasionally from the plural form of Middle English wik(e), late Middle English weke ‘building, especially one where dairy products are made’ (compare Wick ). An alternative Middle English plural form occurs in Wicken .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Wykes was born in 1217, in South Tawton, Devon, England, United Kingdom. She married William Wykes about 1242, in Wyke, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died after 1248.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Wykesは1217年にイギリスイギリスデボンソウス タウトンで生まれた。約1242年にイギリスデボンウイクでWilliam Wykesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1248年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Wykes(은)는 1217년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 소우스 타w턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1242년 영국 데폰 윈이크에서 William Wykes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1248년 이후 사망했다.
